claim: Independent verification confirms explorer claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema correctly supports WebFetch/WebSearch as external evidence (scope=external) with proper separation from project-scoped facts (scope=project requiring path locators). All 12 explorer evidence points independently verified via direct file reads; no conflicts found.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES = new Set(['project', 'external', 'user', 'unknown']) defines four valid scopes with 'external' as a distinct first-class category separate from 'project'
evidence.why: Foundational evidence that scope system explicitly supports external as separate category from project

evidence.summary: Agent prompt conditional: toolProfile.includes('research') enables WebFetch/WebSearch with explicit instruction to mark those records as scope=external and not use them as project facts
evidence.why: Direct confirmation that research profile allows external fetch tools and mandates scope=external marking

evidence.summary: Evidence schema shape requires path field for project scope, allows url field for external scope, and scope field accepts 'project | external | user | unknown'
evidence.why: Schema structure proves external scope has dedicated url field vs path field for project scope

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors function validates every evidence record declares scope as one of four valid values; missing or invalid scope generates error array
evidence.why: Validation enforcement prevents evidence records without explicit scope declarations

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult sets status='blocked', recommendation='block', and error_code='EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED' when evidence lacks valid scope
evidence.why: Runtime enforcement that evidence without proper scope cannot successfully complete

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope function normalizes scope aliases including 'external', 'web', 'internet', 'research' to 'external', and source types like 'external_web', 'webfetch', 'websearch' to external
evidence.why: Scope normalization supports multiple declaration methods for external evidence

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues generates BLOCKED status with PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED or PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE when project facts lack project-scoped evidence with path locators
evidence.why: Hard enforcement that existing-project facts cannot be proven by external/user/unknown evidence alone

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION field evidence states 'scope is project | external | user | unknown; project facts require project-scoped evidence'
evidence.why: Schema contract explicitly separates project facts requiring project evidence from external evidence

evidence.summary: Doctrine states: 'external evidence can come from WebFetch/WebSearch or external docs, but only as background research, cannot alone prove target project existing fields/API/state machine/data flow'
evidence.why: Product-level confirmation that external evidence is allowed but insufficient for project facts

evidence.summary: Unit test 'claude research profile labels research while keeping tools available' confirms agent_tool_profile='research' sets profile and tools remain available
evidence.why: Test evidence that research profile enables external tools without removing other capabilities

evidence.summary: executionConfig normalizes agent_tool_profile: 'research', 'fetch', 'web', 'web-research', 'external-research' all map to 'research' for Claude provider
evidence.why: Profile normalization shows multiple input aliases all enable research mode with external tool access

evidence.summary: Real explorer agent result with 14 evidence records all using scope='project' with exact paths, demonstrating proper project evidence structure in actual practice
evidence.why: Operational evidence that evidence agents correctly structure project-scoped evidence with path locators

assumptions: WebFetch and WebSearch tools are actually provisioned in Claude Code agent environment when agent_tool_profile='research' is set in config
Agents can successfully fetch from https://example.com or other URLs when external research tools are enabled
Boundary snapshot mechanism does not flag external fetch cache artifacts as project mutations
External evidence records marked scope='external' with url field actually pass validation in normalizeAgentResult
risks: If WebFetch/WebSearch tools are not actually available in Claude Code despite research profile being set, external evidence gathering would fail silently
If external fetch operations write cache files to project directory that boundary snapshot detects, legitimate external research would be incorrectly blocked as mutations
If agents misinterpret scope field and mark external fetch results as scope='project', evidence would pass validation but violate separation principle
If research profile normalization in evidence-dispatch.ts:447-459 does not match actual Claude Code tool availability, agents may lack expected external tools
missing: Actual execution log or trace showing WebFetch or WebSearch being successfully used during demand evidence dispatch with scope='external' records
Test cases specifically validating external evidence scope handling with url field (all existing tests use project scope)
Documentation or examples showing proper structure of external evidence records with url field populated
Evidence that boundary snapshot mechanism correctly ignores or allows external fetch cache artifacts in allowed_write_roots
Confirmation that https://example.com or any specific external URL can be successfully fetched during evidence dispatch

result.notes: Independent verification complete: all 12 explorer evidence points confirmed via direct file reads. No conflicts found between explorer claim and source code/docs/tests/artifacts. Implementation correctly separates external research (scope=external with url) from project facts (scope=project with path), satisfying acceptance criteria.
